page: Amazon Web Services c7i.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The configuration provides 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. Memory resources consist of 32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM operating at 5 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations, which accelerate machine learning and vector math workloads. In benchmark evaluations, the c7i.4xlarge demonstrates solid compute capabilities, scoring 10360.0 on Geekbench multi-core and 22580.69 on Passmark CPU tests. Text processing performance is particularly strong, ranking in the top 25% with a Geekbench text processing score of 2321.0.

While the DDR5 memory subsystem achieves a cached read bandwidth of 1158.4 GB/sec, its uncached large-block read and write speeds are 83.57 GB/sec and 57.73 GB/sec, respectively. The Passmark memory score falls into the lower 25% tier, indicating a potential tradeoff for memory-bound applications. However, the server excels in specific tasks, such as small-model LLM text generation, where it achieves a strong 256.18 tokens/sec. It also maintains steady throughput for Redis SET operations at 19.56 million operations per second and static web serving at 26.41 GB/sec.

Given its compute-to-memory ratio of 1:2 and dedicated CPU allocation, the c7i.4xlarge offers high resource density for compute-bound tasks without the overhead of bundled local storage or GPUs. This makes it a cost-efficient choice for workloads that require consistent CPU performance but moderate memory footprints. Recommended applications include software compilation, web serving, text processing, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. The server supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX), which enhance its performance in mathematical and machine learning tasks. Benchmarks show strong capabilities in text processing and small-model LLM text generation, alongside steady web serving throughput of 26.41 GB/sec. While its memory subsystem shows some performance tradeoffs in uncached operations, the instance provides a balanced, cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads. It is highly suitable for software compilation, web serving, and lightweight inference tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Nitro hypervisor virtualization, ideal for compute-heavy workloads.

tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and advanced Intel matrix extensions for intensive processing workloads.

32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz
Supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
